column1: # Curated DHCP fingerprint table — maps a device's DHCP Option 60 (vendor class)
# or Option 55 (parameter request list) to an OS family and/or a device kind.
#   type = vendor       case-insensitive substring on Option 60 (pattern lowercase)
#        | prl          exact match on the Option 55 list ("1,3,6,15,...")
#        | prl_prefix   prefix match on the Option 55 list
#   os   = OS family, or - if unknown
#   kind = device kind (must be one of classify.rs KIND_NAMES), or - if unknown
# Curated from publicly-documented fingerprints — license-clean and safe to
# extend (add rows, or import from an open dataset). Rows are tried top to
# bottom, first match wins, so keep the most specific/reliable first. Vendor
# rows precede PRL rows because Option 60 drifts far less than Option 55 across
# OS versions. Each hit is only one weighted vote in classify.rs, so a strong
# mDNS/SSDP signal still overrides a coarse guess here.
# --- Option 55 parameter request list (version-sensitive; keep conservative) ---
# Apple's signature is the early option 121 (classless static route) right after
# option 1 — distinct from Android ("1,3,6,15...") and Windows ("1,15,3,6...").
# Kept short so it matches across iOS/macOS versions (the tail options drift).
# Android's PRL fallback for phones that omit the Option 60 vendor class. The
# 26,28,51,58,59 run is Android-specific; the prefix diverges early from Apple
# (1,121,…), macOS (1,3,6,15,119,…) and Windows (1,15,3,6,…), so PRL row order
